name: Shakshuka
description: Eggs poached directly in a spiced tomato and pepper sauce — a Middle Eastern breakfast staple eaten straight from the pan.

recipeIngredient.display: Fresh coriander or parsley, to garnish
Crusty bread or pita, to serve

recipeInstructions.text: Heat olive oil in a wide, oven-safe frying pan over medium heat. Fry cumin seeds for 30 seconds until fragrant.
Add onion and pepper. Cook 8 minutes until soft. Add garlic, paprika, and chilli flakes; cook 1 minute.
Pour in tomatoes, season, and simmer for 10 minutes until sauce thickens.
Make 4 wells in the sauce. Crack an egg into each well. Cover and cook over low heat for 5–7 minutes until whites are set but yolks still runny.
Scatter with herbs and serve straight from the pan with bread.

notes.title: Variations
notes.text: Add crumbled feta, merguez sausage, or a dollop of harissa for extra richness.
